info.title: Warcraft Logs v1 API
info.description: The v1 API has been deprecated and is no longer being actively developed. Newer applications should be using the v2 API instead.

get.description: Gets an array of Zone objects. Each zone corresponds to a raid/dungeon instance in the game and has its own set of encounters.
Gets an array of Class objects. Each Class corresponds to a class in the game.

Gets an object that contains a total count and an array of EncounterRanking objects and a total number of rankings for that encounter. Each EncounterRanking corresponds to a single character or guild/team.

parameters.name: encounterID
parameters.description: The encounter to collect rankings for. Encounter IDs can be obtained using a /zones request.

parameters.name: metric
parameters.description: The metric to query for. Valid fight metrics are 'speed', 'execution' and 'feats'. Valid character metrics are 'dps', 'hps', 'bossdps, 'tankhps', or 'playerspeed'. For WoW only, 'krsi' can be used for tank survivability ranks and 'progress' can be used for guild progress info.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The raid size to query for. This is only valid for fixed size raids. Raids with flexible sizing must omit this parameter.

parameters.name: difficulty
parameters.description: The difficulty setting to query for. Valid difficulty settings are 1 = LFR, 2 = Flex, 3 = Normal, 4 = Heroic, 5 = Mythic, 10 = Challenge Mode, 100 = WildStar/FF. Can be omitted for encounters with only one difficulty setting.

parameters.name: partition
parameters.description: The partition group to query for. Most zones have only one partition, and this can be omitted. Hellfire Citadel has two partitions (1 for original, 2 for pre-patch). Highmaul and BRF have two partitions (1 for US/EU, 2 for Asia).

parameters.name: class
parameters.description: The class to query for if a character metric is specified. Valid class IDs can be obtained from a /classes API request. Optional.

parameters.name: spec
parameters.description: The spec to query for if a character metric is specified. Valid spec IDs can be obtained from a /classes API request. Optional.

parameters.name: bracket
parameters.description: The bracket to query for. If omitted or if a value of 0 is specified, then all brackets are examined. Brackets can be obtained from a /zones API request.

parameters.name: server
parameters.description: A server to filter on. If set, the region must also be specified. This is the slug field in Blizzard terminology.

parameters.name: region
parameters.description: The short name of a region to filter on (e.g., US, NA, EU).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page to examine, starting from 1. If the value is omitted, then 1 is assumed. For example, with a page of 2 and a limit of 300, you will be fetching rankings 301-600.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: A search filter string, limiting the search to specific classes, specs, fight durations, raid sizes, etc. The format should match the string used on the public rankings pages.

parameters.name: includeCombatantInfo
parameters.description: Whether or not to include combatant info like gear and talents. Optional. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: excludeLeaderboard
parameters.description: When true, only include ranks that have a backing log. Otherwise include ranks that came from an official leaderboard.

parameters.name: hardModeLevel
parameters.description: Hard mode level for WoW Classic. Ignored if unsupported. -1 for any hard mode (including normal mode), 0 for normal mode, and 1-4 for varying hard modes.

parameters.name: externalBuffs
parameters.description: External buff filter. Most games and zones don't support this. 0 for any externals, 1 to require externals, 2 to exclude externals.

get.description: Gets an array of CharacterRanking objects. Each CharacterRanking corresponds to a single rank on a fight for the specified character.

parameters.name: characterName
parameters.description: The name of the character to collect rankings for.

parameters.name: serverName
parameters.description: The server that the character is found on. For World of Warcraft this is the 'slug' field returned from their realm status API.

parameters.name: serverRegion
parameters.description: The short region name for the server on which the character is located: US, EU, KR, TW, CN.

parameters.name: zone
parameters.description: The zone to fetch rankings for. If omitted, the latest open raid zone is used.

parameters.name: encounter
parameters.description: An encounter within the zone to fetch rankings for. If omitted, all encounters in the zone will be checked.

parameters.name: metric
parameters.description: The metric to query for. Valid character metrics are 'dps', 'hps', 'bossdps, 'tankhps', or 'playerspeed'. For WoW only, 'krsi' can be used for tank survivability ranks.

parameters.name: bracket
parameters.description: The bracket to query for. If omitted or if a value of 0 is specified, then all brackets are examined. Brackets can be obtained from a /zones API request.

parameters.name: partition
parameters.description: The partition group to query for. Most zones have only one partition, and this can be omitted. Hellfire Citadel has two partitions (1 for original, 2 for pre-patch). Highmaul and BRF have two partitions (1 for US/EU, 2 for Asia).

parameters.name: timeframe
parameters.description: Whether to compare against today's rankings or to return historical information (where the rank was back when it was earned. The accepted values are 'today' and 'historical', with the default being 'today'.

parameters.name: includeCombatantInfo
parameters.description: Whether or not to include combatant info like gear and talents. Optional. Defaults to false.

get.description: Obtains all parses for a character in the zone across all specs. Every parse is included and not just rankings.

parameters.name: characterName
parameters.description: The name of the character to collect rankings for.

parameters.name: serverName
parameters.description: The server that the character is found on. For World of Warcraft this is the 'slug' field returned from their realm status API.

parameters.name: serverRegion
parameters.description: The short region name for the server on which the character is located: US, EU, KR, TW, CN.

parameters.name: zone
parameters.description: The zone to fetch rankings for. If omitted, the latest open raid zone is used.

parameters.name: encounter
parameters.description: An encounter within the zone to fetch parses for. If omitted, all encounters in the zone will be checked. If a valid encounter is specified, then historical graph data for the encounter will also be included in the results.

parameters.name: metric
parameters.description: The metric to query for. Valid character metrics are 'dps', 'hps', 'bossdps, 'tankhps', or 'playerspeed'. For WoW only, 'krsi' can be used for tank survivability ranks.

parameters.name: bracket
parameters.description: The bracket to query for. If omitted or if a value of 0 is specified, then all brackets are examined. Brackets can be obtained from a /zones API request. The special value -1 can be used to obtain scores by bracket %, i.e., to automatically look only at the bracket each parse falls in.

parameters.name: compare
parameters.description: Optional. Whether or not to compare against rankings (0) when computing performance percentiles or to compare against statistics (1). A value of 0 is assumed if omitted.

parameters.name: partition
parameters.description: The partition group to query for. Most zones have only one partition, and this can be omitted. Hellfire Citadel has two partitions (1 for original, 2 for pre-patch). Highmaul and BRF have two partitions (1 for US/EU, 2 for Asia).

parameters.name: timeframe
parameters.description: Whether to compare against today's rankings or to return historical information (where the rank was back when it was earned. The accepted values are 'today' and 'historical', with the default being 'today'.

parameters.name: includeCombatantInfo
parameters.description: Whether or not to include combatant info like gear and talents. Optional. Defaults to false.

get.description: Gets an array of Report objects. Each Report corresponds to a single calendar report for the specified guild.

parameters.name: guildName
parameters.description: The name of the guild to collect reports for.

parameters.name: serverName
parameters.description: The server that the guild is found on. For World of Warcraft this is the 'slug' field returned from their realm status API.

parameters.name: serverRegion
parameters.description: The short region name for the server on which the guild is located: US, EU, KR, TW, CN.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: An optional start time. This is a UNIX timestamp but with millisecond precision. If omitted, 0 is assumed.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: An optional end time. This is a UNIX timestamp but with millisecond precision. If omitted, the current time is assumed.

get.description: Gets an array of Report objects. Each Report corresponds to a single calendar report for the specified user's personal logs.

parameters.name: userName
parameters.description: The name of the user to collect reports for.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: An optional start time. This is a UNIX timestamp but with millisecond precision. If omitted, 0 is assumed.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: An optional end time. This is a UNIX timestamp but with millisecond precision. If omitted, the current time is assumed.

get.description: Gets arrays of fights and the participants in those fights. Each Fight corresponds to a single pull of a boss.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: The specific report to collect fights and participants for.

parameters.name: translate
parameters.description: An optional flag indicating that the results should be translated into the language of the host (e.g., cn.warcraftlogs.com would get Chinese results).

get.description: Gets a set of events based off the view you're asking for. This exactly corresponds to the Events view on the site.

parameters.name: view
parameters.description: The type of data requested. Supported values are 'summary', 'damage-done', 'damage-taken', 'healing', 'casts', 'summons', 'buffs', 'debuffs', 'deaths', 'threat', 'resources', 'interrupts' and 'dispels'.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: The specific report to collect table entries for.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: A start time. This is a time from the start of the report in milliseconds. If omitted, 0 is assumed.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: An end time. This is a time from the start of the report in milliseconds. If omitted, 0 is assumed.

parameters.name: hostility
parameters.description: An optional hostility value of 0 or 1. The default is 0. A value of 0 means to collect data for Friendlies. A value of 1 means to collect data for Enemies.

parameters.name: sourceid
parameters.description: An optional actor ID to filter to. If set, only events where the ID matches the source (or target for damage-taken) of the event will be returned. The actor's pets will also be included (unless the options field overrides).

parameters.name: sourceinstance
parameters.description: An optional actor instance ID to filter to. If set, only events where the instance ID matches the source (or target for damage-taken) of the event will be returned. This is useful to look for all events involving NPC N, where N is the actor instance ID.

parameters.name: sourceclass
parameters.description: An optional actor class to filter to. If set, only events where the source (or target for damage-taken) involves that class (e.g., Mage) will be returned.

parameters.name: targetid
parameters.description: An optional actor ID to filter to. If set, only events where the ID matches the target (or source for damage-taken) of the event will be returned. This value is not used in the 'deaths', 'survivability', 'resources' and 'resources-gains' views.

parameters.name: targetinstance
parameters.description: An optional actor instance ID to filter to. If set, only events where the instance ID matches the target (or source for damage-taken) of the event will be returned. This is useful to look for all events involving NPC N, where N is the actor instance ID. This value is not used in the 'deaths', 'survivability', 'resources' and 'resources-gains' views.

parameters.name: targetclass
parameters.description: An optional actor class to filter to. If set, only events where the target (or source for damage-taken) involves that class (e.g., Mage) will be returned. This value is not used in the 'deaths', 'survivability', 'resources' and 'resources-gains' views.

parameters.name: sourceAurasPresent
parameters.description: A comma-separated string of aura game IDs. Only matches if the aura is present on the source.

parameters.name: sourceAurasAbsent
parameters.description: A comma-separated string of aura game IDs. Only matches if the aura is absent on the source.

parameters.name: targetAurasPresent
parameters.description: A comma-separated string of aura game IDs. Only matches if the aura is present on the target.

parameters.name: targetAurasAbsent
parameters.description: A comma-separated string of aura game IDs. Only matches if the aura is absent on the target.

parameters.name: abilityid
parameters.description: An optional ability ID to filter to. If set, only events where the ability matches will be returned. Consolidated abilities (WCL only) are represented using a negative number that matches the ability ID that everything is consolidated under. For the 'deaths' view, this represents a specific killing blow. For the resources views, the abilityid is not an ability but a resource type. Valid resource types can be viewed at https://www.warcraftlogs.com/reports/resource_types/

parameters.name: death
parameters.description: An optional death to filter to. Only used for the deaths command. Select the Nth death in the time range that matches all the other filters.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: A set of options for what to include/exclude. These correspond to options like Include Overkill in the Damage Done pane. Complete list will be forthcoming. If omitted, appropriate defaults that match WCL's default behavior will be chosen. This value is not used in the 'deaths', 'survivability', 'resources' and 'resources-gains' views.

parameters.name: cutoff
parameters.description: An optional death cutoff. If set, events after that number of deaths have occurred will not be examined.

parameters.name: encounter
parameters.description: An optional encounter filter. If set to a specific encounter ID, only fights involving a specific encounter will be considered. The encounter IDs match those used in rankings/statistics.

parameters.name: wipes
parameters.description: An optional wipes filter. If set to 1, only wipes will be considered.

parameters.name: difficulty
parameters.description: An optional difficulty filter.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: An optional filter written in WCL's expression language. Events must match the filter to be included.

parameters.name: translate
parameters.description: An optional flag indicating that the results should be translated into the language of the host (e.g., cn.warcraftlogs.com would get Chinese results).

get.description: Gets a table of entries, either by actor or ability, of damage, healing and cast totals for each entry. This API exactly follows what is returned for the Tables panes on the site. It can and will change as the needs of those panes do, and as such should never be considered a frozen API. Use at your own risk.

parameters.name: view
parameters.description: The type of data requested. Supported values are 'summary', 'damage-done', 'damage-taken', 'healing', 'casts', 'summons', 'buffs', 'debuffs', 'deaths', 'survivability', 'resources' and 'resources-gains'.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: The specific report to collect table entries for.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: A start time. This is a time from the start of the report in milliseconds. If omitted, 0 is assumed.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: An end time. This is a time from the start of the report in milliseconds. If omitted, 0 is assumed.

parameters.name: hostility
parameters.description: An optional hostility value of 0 or 1. The default is 0. A value of 0 means to collect data for Friendlies. A value of 1 means to collect data for Enemies.

parameters.name: by
parameters.description: An optional parameter indicating how to group entries. They can be grouped by 'source', by 'target', or by 'ability'. This value matches WCL's default behavior if omitted. For buffs and debuffs, a value of 'source' means auras gained by the source, and a value of 'target' means auras cast by the source. This value is not used in the 'deaths', 'survivability', 'resources' and 'resources-gains' views.

parameters.name: sourceid
parameters.description: An optional actor ID to filter to. If set, only events where the ID matches the source (or target for damage-taken) of the event will be returned. The actor's pets will also be included (unless the options field overrides).

parameters.name: sourceinstance
parameters.description: An optional actor instance ID to filter to. If set, only events where the instance ID matches the source (or target for damage-taken) of the event will be returned. This is useful to look for all events involving NPC N, where N is the actor instance ID.

parameters.name: sourceclass
parameters.description: An optional actor class to filter to. If set, only events where the source (or target for damage-taken) involves that class (e.g., Mage) will be returned.

parameters.name: targetid
parameters.description: An optional actor ID to filter to. If set, only events where the ID matches the target (or source for damage-taken) of the event will be returned. This value is not used in the 'deaths', 'survivability', 'resources' and 'resources-gains' views.

parameters.name: targetinstance
parameters.description: An optional actor instance ID to filter to. If set, only events where the instance ID matches the target (or source for damage-taken) of the event will be returned. This is useful to look for all events involving NPC N, where N is the actor instance ID. This value is not used in the 'deaths', 'survivability', 'resources' and 'resources-gains' views.

parameters.name: targetclass
parameters.description: An optional actor class to filter to. If set, only events where the target (or source for damage-taken) involves that class (e.g., Mage) will be returned. This value is not used in the 'deaths', 'survivability', 'resources' and 'resources-gains' views.

parameters.name: sourceAurasAbsent
parameters.description: A comma-separated string of aura game IDs. Only matches if the aura is absent on the source.

parameters.name: targetAurasPresent
parameters.description: A comma-separated string of aura game IDs. Only matches if the aura is present on the target.

parameters.name: targetAurasAbsent
parameters.description: A comma-separated string of aura game IDs. Only matches if the aura is absent on the target.

parameters.name: abilityid
parameters.description: An optional ability ID to filter to. If set, only events where the ability matches will be returned. Consolidated abilities (WCL only) are represented using a negative number that matches the ability ID that everything is consolidated under. For the 'deaths' view, this represents a specific killing blow. For the resources views, the abilityid is not an ability but a resource type. Valid resource types can be viewed at https://www.warcraftlogs.com/reports/resource_types/

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: A set of options for what to include/exclude. These correspond to options like Include Overkill in the Damage Done pane. Complete list will be forthcoming. If omitted, appropriate defaults that match WCL's default behavior will be chosen. This value is not used in the 'deaths', 'survivability', 'resources' and 'resources-gains' views.

parameters.name: cutoff
parameters.description: An optional death cutoff. If set, events after that number of deaths have occurred will not be examined.

parameters.name: encounter
parameters.description: An optional encounter filter. If set to a specific encounter ID, only fights involving a specific encounter will be considered. The encounter IDs match those used in rankings/statistics.

parameters.name: wipes
parameters.description: An optional wipes filter. If set to 1, only wipes will be considered.

parameters.name: difficulty
parameters.description: An optional difficulty filter.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: An optional filter written in WCL's expression language. Events must match the filter to be included.

parameters.name: translate
parameters.description: An optional flag indicating that the results should be translated into the language of the host (e.g., cn.warcraftlogs.com would get Chinese results).

id.description: A unique identifier representing this specific zone.

name.description: The English name of the raid zone.

frozen.description: Whether or not the rankings and statistics for the zone are frozen. If set, then the results for this zone are never going to change, and you don't have to fetch them again.

brackets.description: The brackets for this zone. Rankings and statistics are collected for each bracket.

id.description: A unique identifier representing this specific encounter.

name.description: The English name of the encounter.

npcID.description: A unique identifier representing the NPC ID for the zone (WoW Only). Used to feed into WoW's progression API.

id.description: A unique identifier representing this specific rankings bracket.

name.description: An explanation of what the bracket contains.

id.description: A unique identifier representing this specific class.

name.description: The English name of the class.

specs.description: The possible specs for this class.

id.description: A unique identifier representing this specific spec.

name.description: The English name of the spec.

total.description: The total number of rankings given the specified filters.

name.description: The name of the character that obtained the ranking.

total.description: For individual rankings, the DPS/HPS value.

class.description: For individual rankings, the class of the character.

spec.description: For individual rankings, the spec of the character.

guild.description: The name of the guild that obtained the ranking.

server.description: The server that the ranking was obtained on.

region.description: The short name of the region that the server belongs to.

duration.description: The length of the fight/run in milliseconds.

startTime.description: A timestamp with millisecond precision that indicates when the fight/run happened.

damageTaken.description: For fight rankings, how much damage was taken over the course of the fight.

deaths.description: For fight rankings, how much people died on the fight.

itemLevel.description: For fight rankings, the average item level of the raid. For character rankings, the item level of the character.

patch.description: For challenge mode rankings, what patch they were obtained in.

reportID.description: The report ID that contains this ranking.

fightID.description: The fight ID within the report for the fight that contains this ranking.

team.description: For challenge mode rankings, the team members that made up the team.

size.description: The size of the raid. Only set for flexible size raids. Remember that flexible raid sizes are all ranked together currently.

name.description: The name of the character on the team.

class.description: The class of the character on the team.

spec.description: The spec of the character on the team.

rank.description: The rank achieved by the character.

outOf.description: The total number of parses. You can use the 'rank' and 'outOf' fields to determine a percentage.

total.description: For individual rankings, the DPS/HPS value.

class.description: For individual rankings, the class of the character.

spec.description: For individual rankings, the spec of the character.

guild.description: The name of the guild that the character belonged to when they obtained the ranking.

duration.description: The length of the fight/run in milliseconds.

startTime.description: A timestamp with millisecond precision that indicates when the fight/run happened.

itemLevel.description: For fight rankings, the average item level of the raid. For character rankings, the item level of the character.

patch.description: For challenge mode rankings, what patch they were obtained in.

reportID.description: The report ID that contains this ranking.

fightID.description: The fight ID within the report for the fight that contains this ranking.

difficulty.description: The difficulty setting that this rank was for.

size.description: The size of the raid. Remember that flexible raid sizes are all ranked together currently.

estimated.description: Whether or not this ranking was estimated (if it was outside the cutoff limit of 500).

id.description: The report code. Can be viewed on the site with the URL string of /reports/<code>.

owner.description: The name of the user that uploaded the report.

zone.description: The zone that the report is for. A value of -1 indicates that the zone for the report is not known.

startTime.description: A UNIX timestamp with millisecond precision, indicating the start time of the report as far as logged events.

endTime.description: A UNIX timestamp with millisecond precision, indicating the end time of the report as far as logged events.
